name: mongosh
context: Use mongosh for interactive inspection, scripted admin tasks, and ad hoc MongoDB troubleshooting
info: This skill packages the official MongoDB Shell workflow: connect to Atlas or self-managed deployments, run JavaScript with `--eval` or `--file`, start with `--nodb` when the script performs its own connection, and use the shell to inspect collections, run commands, or apply targeted maintenance. It matters because mongosh is the fastest path from a live question to a real answer when an agent needs direct observability rather than a full driver-based program.
triggers: inspect a mongodb collection from the shell
body:
# mongosh

`mongosh` is the official MongoDB shell. Use it when you need fast inspection, scripted maintenance, or a verified shell-side reproduction of a problem before writing driver code.

## Connect and Inspect

```bash
mongosh "$MONGODB_URI" --quiet
mongosh "$MONGODB_URI" --apiVersion 1 --username "$MONGODB_USERNAME"
```

Inside the shell:

```javascript
show dbs
use app
db.users.find({ role: "admin" }).limit(5)
db.runCommand({ ping: 1 })
```

## Execute a Script File

```bash
mongosh "$MONGODB_URI" --file ./scripts/audit-users.js
mongosh --nodb --file ./scripts/connect-inside-script.js
```

When a script performs its own `connect()` call, use `--nodb` so mongosh does not attempt an implicit local connection first.

## Execute Inline JavaScript

```bash
mongosh "$MONGODB_URI" --quiet --eval "db.getSiblingDB("app").users.countDocuments({ active: true })"
```

Use [[mongodb/crud]] for structured read/write automation and [[mongodb/aggregation]] for complex reporting flows. Use `mongosh` when you need direct shell affordances, admin commands, or a quick confirmatory check against live data.

sections.title: Connect and select a database
sections.body: Connect with `mongosh "$MONGODB_URI"` and then use `db.getSiblingDB("name")` or `use name` in interactive mode. For Atlas, SRV URIs are typical. For self-managed deployments, a standard URI may include multiple hosts, `replicaSet`, and `authSource`. See [[mongodb/auth#connection-uris]].

sections.title: Run files and inline code
sections.body: Use `--file` for reusable scripts and `--eval` for short shell expressions. If the script contains its own `connect()` call, pass `--nodb`. Inside a running shell you can load another file with `load("./script.js")`. This makes mongosh suitable for deterministic CI checks and one-off admin actions.

sections.title: Inspection workflow
sections.body: A reliable shell workflow is: `ping`, switch DB, sample documents with `find().limit()`, inspect indexes with `getIndexes()`, then run a focused command or aggregation. This sequence avoids writing code before you understand the current state of the deployment.

links.context: Connection strings and auth setup for mongosh
Move from shell exploration to structured CRUD automation
Inspect and troubleshoot indexes after connecting

related.note: mongosh still needs a valid URI, reachable deployment, and correct role bindings
Use the driver skill when you need machine-friendly JSON input and output rather than ad hoc shell work

scripts.name: Run mongosh command or file
scripts.description: Execute an inline expression or a script file through mongosh using environment variables for the connection and mode

MONGODB_URI.description: MongoDB connection string. Optional only when MONGOSH_NODB=1.

MONGOSH_EVAL.description: Inline JavaScript expression to execute with --eval

MONGOSH_FILE.description: Path to a JavaScript file to execute with --file

MONGOSH_NODB.description: Set to 1 to start with --nodb because the script handles connect() itself

MONGOSH_QUIET.description: Set to 0 to keep shell startup noise enabled

MONGOSH_JSON.description: Optional mongosh --json mode such as canonical or relaxed

MONGOSH_API_VERSION.description: Optional Stable API version flag for Atlas or API-stable workloads

uri.description: MongoDB connection string. Optional when mode uses --nodb and the script connects internally.

file.description: Path to a JavaScript file to run with --file

nodb.description: Start without an implicit connection because the script calls connect() itself

quiet.description: Suppress the shell banner and connection chatter

jsonMode.description: Optional JSON formatting mode for mongosh output

mode.description: Which mongosh execution path was used

stdout.description: Shell output emitted by the command or script

depends.reason: URI structure, roles, and TLS settings must be correct before invoking mongosh

author: dojo-community
